comparability_notes: Comparable in absolute volume. Methods vary (rooftop harvesting, check dams, etc.).
canonical_definition: Volume of water recharged to groundwater or surface water sources through conservation and harvesting initiatives.
Total volume of water discharged from operations to all destinations including surface water, groundwater, seawater, and third parties.

comparability_notes: Comparable as share of total energy. Bio-based fuel types may differ by company.
canonical_definition: Share of total energy consumption sourced from bio-based or biomass fuels.
Total absolute energy consumed from renewable sources including solar, wind, hydro, and biomass.
Total absolute energy consumed from non-renewable sources including coal, oil, natural gas, and grid electricity from fossil fuel generation.

comparability_notes: Comparable only when denominator unit (tonne, kg, litre, case) is stated and consistent. Cross-company comparison requires normalisation.
aliases: energy intensity in terms of physical output

external_refs.label: Energy intensity per unit of physical output

canonical_definition: Energy consumed per unit of physical production output, e.g. GJ per tonne produced.
Installed capacity of rooftop solar photovoltaic plants across company facilities, typically in KWp or MW.

aliases: post consumer plastic collected and recycled

external_refs.label: Waste diverted from disposal â€” recycled

canonical_definition: Weight of post-consumer plastic waste collected and recycled under extended producer responsibility (EPR) or voluntary schemes.
Total plastic waste generated from operations.

comparability_notes: Comparable only when denominator unit is consistent.
aliases: waste intensity in terms of physical output

external_refs.label: Waste intensity per unit of physical output

canonical_definition: Total waste generated per unit of physical production output.
Total waste diverted from disposal through recycling, reuse, and other recovery operations. Recycled / Re-used / Other recovery are dimensional breakdowns (recovery_method dimension).
dimension_note: Add dimension recovery_method: recycled | reused | other_recovery

canonical_definition: Total waste directed to disposal including landfill and incineration. Incineration / Landfilling are dimensional breakdowns (disposal_method dimension).
dimension_note: Add dimension disposal_method: incineration | landfilling | other_disposal

canonical_definition: Total nitrogen oxide (NOx) emissions from operations.
Total sulphur oxide (SOx) emissions from operations.

comparability_notes: Comparable as absolute savings. Baseline methodology may differ.
canonical_definition: Total energy saved through conservation and efficiency initiatives during the reporting period.

comparability_notes: Comparable as absolute savings. Baseline methodology may differ.
canonical_definition: Total water saved through conservation and efficiency initiatives during the reporting period.

comparability_notes: Distinct from water withdrawal. Consumption = withdrawal minus discharge returned to source.
canonical_definition: Total water consumed by operations, net of water returned to source. Distinct from water withdrawal.
Volume of rainwater collected and harvested through rooftop or other collection systems.

comparability_notes: Not comparable across companies due to different scopes and accounting.
canonical_definition: Capital or operating expenditure on water conservation and efficiency initiatives.

comparability_notes: Not comparable across companies.
aliases: total expense on energy savings initiatives
canonical_definition: Capital or operating expenditure on energy conservation and efficiency initiatives.
Total waste sent to landfill. Distinct from total waste disposed (which includes incineration).
note: waste_disposed_by_landfill is a dimension under waste_disposed_total. This canonical is for the standalone total when reported without method breakdown.

comparability_notes: Comparable when recyclability definition is consistent (design for recyclability vs certified recyclable).
canonical_definition: Percentage of plastic packaging that is designed to be recyclable.
Total weight of plastic packaging material consumed or used in products.

comparability_notes: Comparable when denominator unit is consistent.
canonical_definition: Plastic packaging used per unit of physical production output.

display_name: Total Plastic Recycled (Pre and Post Consumer)
canonical_definition: Total plastic waste recycled including both pre-consumer (manufacturing) and post-consumer sources.
note: Distinct from post_consumer_plastic_recycled which covers only post-consumer. Use this when the company reports a combined figure.

display_name: Water Withdrawal from High Water-Stress Areas
comparability_notes: Comparable when stress area classification (WRI Aqueduct or equivalent) is consistent.
aliases: water withdrawal from high water-stress areas
canonical_definition: Share or volume of water withdrawn from locations identified as high or extremely high water-stressed areas.

comparability_notes: Not directly comparable. Scope 3 boundary and category coverage varies significantly by company.
canonical_definition: Total indirect greenhouse gas emissions from value chain activities (Scope 3), covering categories reported by the company.
Total electronic waste generated from operations.

aliases: water discharge to surface water with treatment

external_refs.label: Water discharged - surface water, with treatment

canonical_definition: Volume of water discharged to surface water bodies after treatment.
Total construction and demolition waste generated from operations.

aliases: pre-consumer plastic packaging waste recycling rate
canonical_definition: Percentage of pre-consumer (manufacturing scrap) plastic packaging waste that is recycled.
Total weight of virgin plastic eliminated through use of recycled content or material substitution.

comparability_notes: Comparable in absolute terms. Baseline and methodology for calculating avoidance may differ.
canonical_definition: Total greenhouse gas emissions avoided through energy efficiency, renewable energy, or other initiatives.

comparability_notes: Comparable when denominator unit is consistent.
canonical_definition: Water consumed per unit of physical production output.

comparability_notes: Comparable when denominator (total waste generated) is consistent.

external_refs.label: Waste diverted from disposal â€” percentage

canonical_definition: Percentage of total waste generated that is recycled or recovered.

display_name: Recycled Waste Paper Used as Input
canonical_definition: Weight of post-consumer waste paper collected and used as raw material input in paperboard or packaging production.
note: ITC-specific given its paper/paperboard business, but reusable for any company reporting recycled fibre inputs.

canonical_definition: Number of operational units achieving zero liquid discharge status.

aliases: installed solar and wind power capacity
canonical_definition: Total installed capacity of renewable power generation assets including solar and wind, in MW or KWp.
note: Broader than rooftop_solar_installed_capacity â€” use when company reports combined solar+wind or any renewable capacity figure.

canonical_definition: Weight of wet or organic waste diverted from disposal through composting.

comparability_notes: Comparable in absolute volume. Methodology for estimating storage potential may differ.
canonical_definition: Total net water storage potential created through rainwater harvesting structures and water conservation initiatives.

comparability_notes: Not directly comparable â€” crop type and growing conditions vary significantly.
canonical_definition: Water used per unit of agricultural output, measuring efficiency of water use in farming operations.

comparability_notes: Comparable using CDP's standardised scoring methodology.
canonical_definition: Score awarded by CDP (formerly Carbon Disclosure Project) for water security disclosure and management.

aliases: number of complaints filed during the year
complaints filed during the year from employees and workers
complaints filed during the year from customers
complaints filed during the year from shareholders
complaints filed during the year from value chain partners
complaints filed during the year from communities
complaints filed during the year from investors
complaints pending at close of year
complaints pending resolution at close of the year from employees and workers
complaints pending resolution at close of the year from customers
complaints pending resolution at close of the year from shareholders
complaints pending resolution at close of the year from value chain partners
complaints pending resolution at close of the year from communities
complaints pending resolution at close of the year from investors
ghg intensity per tonne of production
total scope 1 and scope 2 emission intensity
total scope 2 emissions gross including irecs net
return to work rate male permanent employees
return to work rate female permanent employees
employees returned to work after parental leave

display_name: Health and Safety Practices Assessment Coverage
aliases: percentage assessed for health and safety
health and safety practices assessed percentage
workers assessed for health and safety
waste water generation per ton of production
waste water generation for every ton of production
reduction in total scope 1 and 2 emissions

comparability_notes: BRSR Principle 3 mandatory indicator. Comparable across companies. Rate expressed per million hours worked.
aliases: lost time injury frequency rate (ltifr) workers
canonical_definition: Number of lost time injuries per million hours worked by workers.

comparability_notes: BRSR Principle 3 mandatory indicator.
canonical_definition: Total number of recordable work-related injuries for workers in the reporting period.

comparability_notes: BRSR Principle 3 mandatory indicator.
canonical_definition: Total number of recordable work-related injuries for employees in the reporting period.

display_name: Permanent Workers in Association or Union
comparability_notes: BRSR Principle 3 mandatory indicator.
aliases: permanent workers in association(s) or union
permanent workers in associations or union
canonical_definition: Number of permanent workers who are members of trade unions or worker associations.

display_name: Total Fuel Consumption from Non-Renewable Sources
comparability_notes: BRSR Principle 6 essential. Fuel only (excludes purchased electricity).
aliases: total fuel consumption from non-renewable sources (e)
canonical_definition: Total fuel consumed from non-renewable sources (coal, diesel, furnace oil, etc.) in GJ.

display_name: Total Fuel Consumption from Renewable Sources
comparability_notes: BRSR Principle 6 essential. Fuel only (excludes purchased electricity).
aliases: total fuel consumption from renewable sources (b)
canonical_definition: Total fuel consumed from renewable sources (biomass, biogas, etc.) in GJ.

display_name: Water Discharge Sent to Third Parties with Treatment
comparability_notes: BRSR Principle 6 essential.
aliases: water discharge sent to third-parties with treatment
water discharged to third party treated
water discharge to third parties (with treatment)
canonical_definition: Volume of water discharged to third parties after treatment.

display_name: Male Employees Trained on Health and Safety
comparability_notes: BRSR Principle 3 essential gender-disaggregated indicator.
aliases: employees male on health and safety measures
employees (male) covered under health & safety measures
canonical_definition: Number of male employees trained on or covered under health and safety measures.

comparability_notes: BRSR Principle 3 mandatory indicator.
aliases: high consequence work-related injury or ill-health employees
high consequence work related injuries employees
canonical_definition: Number of high consequence work-related injuries or ill-health cases for employees.
